cards.principleAPrediction: Accessible Falsifier Boundary: allow or caveat only if a direct falsifier can be executed on the public artifact.
cards.principleBPrediction: Resolution-Cost Gradient: downgrade when the missing evidence requires high-cost or unavailable resolution; caveat when resolution is cheap.
cards.principleCPrediction: Executable Specificity Dominance: narrow executable claims can survive with fewer descriptive fields if the behavior is directly runnable.
cards.principleDPrediction: Negative-Control Asymmetry: failing or decisive negative controls dominate documentation and replay fields.
cards.existingPrinciplePrediction: Existing Required Evidence Field Boundary: missing required fields force caveat, downgrade, block, or not-testable labels.
cards.whyNonObvious: AFB predicts caveated allowance because direct label-shuffle falsifier is accessible; existing field-bound logic predicts downgrade for absent official protocol.
cards.falsifier: Observed execution contradicts the named principle while a rival predicts the observation with fewer caveats.

cards.replayPlan: repeat retrieval or runtime smoke in the same isolated execution context; document failure if replay cannot run
cards.noEditRule: This card is frozen before execution; only observation files may be appended later.
